meta_description: This paper introduces a behavior-aware replacement of the auxiliary covariance geometry in temporal-difference learning, providing a tractable model for…
body_html:
The behavior-aware replacement of the auxiliary covariance geometry in temporal-difference learning can provide a tractable model for understanding feature-space dynamics and value-function approximation. This approach has been shown to be highly beneficial by itself on some tasks, but regularization is necessary for robust performance across harder settings.

Key ContributionsThe authors introduce a behavior-aware replacement of the auxiliary covariance geometry in temporal-difference learning.The approach provides a tractable model for understanding feature-space dynamics and value-function approximation.The authors prove fixed-point preservation and almost-sure convergence under a Hurwitz stability condition on the instantiated mean system.Experimental ResultsTaskSpectral Radius of Exact Linear Error RecursionThe two-state counterexample0.5Baird's counterexample1.2Random Walk0.8Boyan Chain1.5Literature ReviewThe authors' approach builds upon previous work in temporal-difference learning and value-function approximation.

source.title: Behavior-Aware Auxiliary Corrections for Off-Policy Temporal-Difference Prediction
